extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 8.6km Turin ITT by 10 seconds over AFFINI, crushing specialists at his home-region race.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma placed two riders (FOSS, AFFINI) in top positions before GANNA, revealing exceptional time trial depth across roster.
CAVAGNA flopped despite winning Swiss ITT week prior, suggesting short preparation races unreliable for Giro form prediction.
EVENEPOEL sixth at 21yo in Grand Tour debut after 2020 injury, signaling immediate GC threat capability post-recovery.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: demolished field by 10 seconds on home roads, confirming regional/motivational advantage multiplies existing TT superiority
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: positive: first-year Jumbo recruit beat established names, suggesting team's TT development program extracts immediate gains
rider_signals.FOSS Tobias: positive: led briefly despite non-TT specialist background, indicating Jumbo's depth threatens even non-obvious stages
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: negative: disappointed despite recent Swiss ITT win, revealing peak timing issues or insufficient Giro-specific preparation
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: very high placement in Giro debut after 2020 abandonment, signals genuine GC readiness over previous year's failure
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: sixth place at age 21 in first Grand Tour start post-injury indicates no residual weakness from 2020 crash
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: only 7.3 seconds down after spending 15 days in pink 2020, confirms repeatable TT competence for GC contention
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: just satisfactory for Tour winner, suggesting either sandbagging early or genuine ITT limitations persisting
